results.rendered_html: yayāti m. (prob. fr. √yat) N. of a celebrated monarch of the lunar race (son of king Nahuṣa whom he succeeded ; from his two wives came the two lines of the lunar race, Yadu being the son of Devayānī, daughter of Uśanas or Śukra, and Puru of Śarmiṣṭhā, daughter of Vṛṣa-parvan; Yayāti Nāhuṣa is also represented as the author of  RV. ix, 101, 4 -6 ),  RV. ;  MaitrUp. ;  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.

I, 713 . 726 . yayāterye nahuṣyasya barhiṣidevā āsate  ṚV. 10,63,1 . yayātivat 1,31,17 . —  ṚV.
